---
title: "Graph-based analysis of multiplex immunofluorescence"
description: "Graph Neural Networks over multi-channel images, capturing spatial cell-cell relationships in multiplex IF for downstream phenotype classification."
date: 2021-06-01
categories: [Professional, Drug Discovery, Graph Learning]
image: ../assets/img/placeholder-square.png
---

::: {.eyebrow}
AstraZeneca · Drug Discovery ML
:::

## Problem

Multiplex immunofluorescence (mIF) images carry rich information about cell
type, marker expression, and — crucially — **spatial relationships between
cells**. Conventional analysis pipelines flatten this to per-cell feature
tables, discarding the spatial structure that often correlates most directly
with biological mechanism and clinical outcome.

## Approach

Treat mIF images as graphs. Each cell becomes a node with marker-expression
features; spatial proximity defines edges. Graph Neural Networks then learn
representations that explicitly model cell-neighborhood interactions for
downstream phenotype classification.

## Result

The approach is described in a preprint on bioRxiv and was presented at SITC
2021 and at the AACR Tumor Immunology and Immunotherapy 2021 meeting:

> Innocenti C, **Zhang Z**, Selvaraj B, Gaffney I, Frangos M, Cohen-Setton J, et al.
> "An unsupervised graph embeddings approach to multiplex immunofluorescence image exploration."
> *bioRxiv* 2021.06.09.447654 (preprint).

[bioRxiv preprint](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2021.06.09.447654v1){.external} ·
[SITC 2021 abstract](https://jitc.bmj.com/content/9/Suppl_2/A860){.external} ·
[AACR Clinical Cancer Research abstract](https://aacrjournals.org/clincancerres/article/27/5_Supplement/PR-05/32768){.external}

## Stack

PyTorch Geometric, scikit-learn, custom mIF preprocessing pipeline.
